title: At TechForward, Vasquez Defends Volta's Acquisition Strategy
body:
Elena Vasquez took the stage in Amsterdam on Thursday looking less like someone who had just started a new job two weeks ago and more like someone who had been running Volta Systems for years.

The newly appointed CTO used her keynote slot at TechForward to make a direct case for why the German quantum startup's purchase of Dutch chipmaker LuminCore is not an anticompetitive move but a survival necessity. "If we don't own our stack, we're building on sand," she said to a packed auditorium.

The comments come as EU competition regulators are conducting a preliminary review of the deal. Vasquez did not name the Commission directly but acknowledged that "scrutiny is appropriate" before brushing past the topic.

She spent the bulk of her talk on Volta's processor architecture, revealing that the next generation system — internally called Atlas — would achieve coherence times roughly three times longer than the current platform. No commercial availability date was given.

Dr. Priya Sharma, attending the conference in a different capacity, was spotted in the front row during Vasquez's session. The two appeared to speak briefly afterward, though neither elaborated on the nature of that conversation.
